headline: How Interest Rates Shape Economic Activity
reading:
Interest rates are one of the most important tools used by central banks to influence economic activity. By adjusting rates, policymakers can encourage or discourage borrowing, which in turn affects spending, investment, and overall growth.

When interest rates are low, borrowing becomes cheaper. Businesses are more likely to invest in new projects, and consumers are more willing to take loans for houses, cars, or other purchases. As a result, economic activity tends to increase.

However, low rates can also lead to excessive borrowing and rising prices. When too much money flows into the economy, inflation may accelerate.

On the other hand, higher interest rates make borrowing more expensive. This can slow down spending and reduce inflationary pressure.

In this way, interest rates act as a balancing mechanism in the economy.

sentences.ko: 금리는 경제 활동을 조절하는 중요한 도구이다.
sentences.en: Interest rates are an important tool used to influence economic activity.
When interest rates fall, borrowing and spending increase.

sentences.ko: 높은 금리는 인플레이션을 억제할 수 있다.
sentences.en: Higher interest rates can reduce inflation.

quiz.q: What is the main function of interest rates?
What happens when interest rates are low?
What risk comes from low interest rates?
What do high interest rates do?

summary: Interest rates affect economic (activity) by influencing borrowing and spending. Low rates can increase (inflation), while high rates help stabilize (prices).
